headlines.title: WEEK'S STOCK MARKET CLOSES WITH RISE IN UNITED STATES STEEL

headlines.title: Cotton Bulls Hold Position; Finals Show Further Gains

headlines.title: WHEAT RULES FIRM WITH FAIR SUPPORT; SHORTS BUY FREELY
headlines.subtitle: Reports of Damage by Hessian Fly Causes Uneasiness Among Traders.

headlines.title: Practically No Market on Saturday in Cattle Yards
headlines.subtitle: Receipts Limited to 58 Calves Supply of 700 Hogs Is Disposed of at Steady Prices, With Three Cars Notching $8,35 for a Top No Sheep.

headlines.title: COFFEE TRADE DULL; CONSUMPTION DEMAND SHOWS BIG DECREASE
headlines.subtitle: Strawberry Supply More Liberal and of Better Quality. Poultry Steady.

headlines.title: SECURITIES TAKE ON MIDSUMMER DULLNESS
headlines.subtitle: Crop Prospects Continue Favorable and Mexican Situation Helped to Hold Prices.

headlines.title: FREE MILK AND ICE WORKERS MEET MONDAY
headlines.subtitle: Members of Sub - Committee Will Be Announced in Star-Telegram Sunday.

headlines.title: ROTARIANS FROLIC IN STUNTS AT LAKE ERIE
headlines.subtitle: Barbecue, Vaudeville, Dancing and Song Feature First Outing of Season.

headlines.title: THREE FILE CLAIMS ON DAMAGE TO LAND
headlines.subtitle: Overflow at Lake Werth Basis for Additional Actions Against City.

headlines.title: FIVE WOMEN ESCAPE JAIL; THREE CAUGHT
headlines.subtitle: Negroes Climb Thrugh Window to Two Make Get-Away.

headlines.title: WOMEN FRIGHTENED BY MEXICAN; MAN FINED
